summary: chain-api-sdk impersonates multichain-crypto-wallet and routes wallet operations through lookalike dependency mchain-sdk
details: The package presents itself as the `iamnotstatic/multichain-crypto-wallet` wallet library — README, badges, repository, homepage, and the shipped `src/` tree all reference that project. However, the published entrypoint `dist/chain-api-sdk.cjs.*.js` does not execute the shipped `src/` code; the compiled bundle only re-exports `mci` and `multiChainInterface` from a runtime dependency named `mchain-sdk` (declared in `package.json` as `"mchain-sdk": "^4.2.5"`). The `mchain-sdk` name is not mentioned anywhere in the README and closely resembles the impersonated `multichain-crypto-wallet` name. Developers following the documented API (`generateMnemonic`, `createWallet`, `transfer`, `getEncryptedJsonFromPrivateKey`) will hand seed phrases, private keys, and wallet passwords to code loaded from an undocumented lookalike dependency. The shipped source tree functions as cover; the actual runtime is the lookalike package. Installing chain-api-sdk silently pulls `mchain-sdk` into the installer's dependency tree, where any wallet-sensitive material provided to the documented API is processed.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
